---
title: "3SB - Offense Report"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 2
    css: styles.css
    code-tools: false
    fig-align: left
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path

# Seaborn native theme - keep grids and default palette
sns.set_theme(
    style="whitegrid",
    palette="deep",
    rc={
        "font.family": "Georgia, serif",
        "font.serif": ["Georgia"],
        "axes.titlesize": 12,
        "axes.titleweight": "normal",
        "axes.titlelocation": "left",
        "axes.labelsize": 10,
        "axes.edgecolor": "#cccccc",
        "grid.color": "#eeeeee",
        "axes.spines.top": False,
        "axes.spines.right": False,
        "figure.dpi": 110,
    },
)

CSV = Path("../3sb_passes.csv")
df = pd.read_csv(CSV)

FIELD_W = 1000
FIELD_H = 467
EZ_DEPTH = 164
INFIELD_W = FIELD_W - 2 * EZ_DEPTH
HUCK_THRESHOLD = 0.5 * INFIELD_W

def normalize(d):
    # 180° rotation for left-attacking points so all passes are viewed
    # from the offense's perspective (attack to the right).
    d = d.copy()
    mask = d["pass_direction"] == "left"
    d.loc[mask, "x1"] = FIELD_W - d.loc[mask, "x1"]
    d.loc[mask, "x2"] = FIELD_W - d.loc[mask, "x2"]
    d.loc[mask, "y1"] = FIELD_H - d.loc[mask, "y1"]
    d.loc[mask, "y2"] = FIELD_H - d.loc[mask, "y2"]
    d.loc[mask, "pass_direction"] = "right"
    return d

df = normalize(df)

df["dx"] = df["x2"] - df["x1"]
df["dy"] = df["y2"] - df["y1"]
df["dist"] = np.sqrt(df["dx"] ** 2 + df["dy"] ** 2)
df["is_forward"] = df["dx"] > 0
df["is_huck"] = df["dx"].abs() >= HUCK_THRESHOLD
df["is_score"] = (~df["is_turnover"].astype(bool)) & (df["x2"] > FIELD_W - EZ_DEPTH)
df["comment_lower"] = df["comment"].fillna("").str.lower()
df["is_mechanical"] = df["comment_lower"].str.contains("mechanic", regex=True)

def point_summary(g):
    g = g.sort_values("pass_seq")
    n_to = int(g["is_turnover"].sum())
    last_is_to = bool(g["is_turnover"].iloc[-1])
    n_poss = n_to if last_is_to else n_to + 1
    scored = bool(g["is_score"].any())
    return pd.Series({
        "match_opponent": g["match_opponent"].iloc[0],
        "turnovers": n_to,
        "possessions": n_poss,
        "scored": scored,
        "broken": not scored,
        "had_turnover": n_to > 0,
        "n_passes": len(g),
    })

points = df.groupby("point_id", as_index=False).apply(point_summary, include_groups=False)
points = points.reset_index(drop=True)

def match_summary(g):
    return pd.Series({
        "points": len(g),
        "scored": int(g["scored"].sum()),
        "broken": int(g["broken"].sum()),
        "total_turnovers": int(g["turnovers"].sum()),
        "total_possessions": int(g["possessions"].sum()),
        "ppp": g["scored"].sum() / g["possessions"].sum() if g["possessions"].sum() else 0,
        "tpp": g["turnovers"].mean(),
        "break_rate": g["broken"].mean(),
    })

matches = points.groupby("match_opponent", as_index=False).apply(match_summary, include_groups=False)
order = matches.sort_values("points", ascending=False)["match_opponent"].tolist()

# Hard-coded results - inferred outcomes can be wrong because not every point is recorded.
MATCH_RESULTS = {
    "Mooncatchers": "loss",
    "Grut": "loss",
    "Iznogood": "loss",
    "Thac": "win",
    "Wall City": "win",
}
WIN_COLOR = "#2e7d32"
LOSS_COLOR = "#c62828"
result_palette = {
    opp: (WIN_COLOR if MATCH_RESULTS.get(opp) == "win" else LOSS_COLOR)
    for opp in order
}
```

```{python}
#| label: field-helper
def draw_field(ax):
    ax.set_xlim(0, FIELD_W)
    ax.set_ylim(FIELD_H, 0)
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    ax.set_facecolor("white")
    ax.add_patch(plt.Rectangle((0, 0), FIELD_W, FIELD_H, fill=False, edgecolor="#1a1a1a", linewidth=1.2))
    ax.axvline(EZ_DEPTH, color="#1a1a1a", linewidth=0.8)
    ax.axvline(FIELD_W - EZ_DEPTH, color="#1a1a1a", linewidth=0.8)
    ax.annotate("", xy=(FIELD_W/2 + 35, -12), xytext=(FIELD_W/2 - 35, -12),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color="#888"),
                annotation_clip=False)
    ax.text(FIELD_W/2, -28, "offense", ha="center", fontsize=9, color="#888")
    ax.grid(False)
    ax.set_xticks([]); ax.set_yticks([])
    for s in ax.spines.values(): s.set_visible(False)
```

# Match-level statistics
All of the statistics are only for the O1 line, not O2 or other rotations.

## Points played per match

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=matches, x="match_opponent", y="points", order=order, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="points played", title="Points per match")
ax.yaxis.set_major_locator(plt.matplotlib.ticker.MaxNLocator(integer=True))
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Turnovers per match

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=matches, x="match_opponent", y="total_turnovers", order=order,
            hue="match_opponent", palette=result_palette, legend=False, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="turnovers", title="Turnovers per match")
ax.yaxis.set_major_locator(plt.matplotlib.ticker.MaxNLocator(integer=True))
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    v = matches.loc[matches["match_opponent"] == opp, "total_turnovers"].iloc[0]
    ax.text(i, v + 0.1, f"{int(v)}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Pass completion rate per match

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
completion = df.groupby("match_opponent", as_index=False).agg(
    passes=("pass_id", "count"),
    turnovers=("is_turnover", "sum"),
)
completion["completion_rate"] = 1 - completion["turnovers"] / completion["passes"]
completion = pd.DataFrame({"match_opponent": order}).merge(completion, on="match_opponent", how="left")

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=completion, x="match_opponent", y="completion_rate", order=order,
            hue="match_opponent", palette=result_palette, legend=False, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="completion rate", title="Pass completion rate per match")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    row = completion.loc[completion["match_opponent"] == opp].iloc[0]
    ax.text(i, row["completion_rate"] + 0.01,
            f"{row['completion_rate']:.0%} ({int(row['passes'] - row['turnovers'])}/{int(row['passes'])})",
            ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Scoring efficiency - points per possession
Possession: a series of passes until a turnover.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=matches, x="match_opponent", y="ppp", order=order,
            hue="match_opponent", palette=result_palette, legend=False, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="points / possession",
       title="Conversion rate - how often a possession results in a score")
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    v = matches.loc[matches["match_opponent"] == opp, "ppp"].iloc[0]
    ax.text(i, v + 0.01, f"{v:.2f}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Passes per successful possession

Average number of passes in a possession that ended in a score.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
def successful_possession_passes(g):
    g = g.sort_values("pass_seq")
    if not g["is_score"].any():
        return None
    # last turnover index within the point, -1 if none
    to_idx = g.index[g["is_turnover"] == 1]
    start = (to_idx.max() + 1) if len(to_idx) else g.index.min()
    return len(g.loc[start:])

succ = df.groupby("point_id").apply(successful_possession_passes, include_groups=False)
succ = succ.dropna().rename("passes").reset_index()
succ = succ.merge(df[["point_id", "match_opponent"]].drop_duplicates(),
                  on="point_id")
agg = succ.groupby("match_opponent", as_index=False)["passes"].mean()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=agg, x="match_opponent", y="passes", order=order,
            hue="match_opponent", palette=result_palette, legend=False, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="passes / successful possession",
       title="Average passes in possessions that ended in a score")
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    row = agg[agg["match_opponent"] == opp]
    if len(row):
        v = row["passes"].iloc[0]
        ax.text(i, v + 0.1, f"{v:.1f}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Break rate
Percentage of O points that ended in a break per match.  

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=matches, x="match_opponent", y="break_rate", order=order,
            hue="match_opponent", palette=result_palette, legend=False, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="break rate", title="Break rate per match")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    v = matches.loc[matches["match_opponent"] == opp, "break_rate"].iloc[0]
    ax.text(i, v + 0.01, f"{v:.0%}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Clean holds

Percentage of points where we scored without a single turnover.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
points["clean_hold"] = points["scored"] & (points["turnovers"] == 0)
clean = points.groupby("match_opponent", as_index=False)["clean_hold"].mean()
clean = pd.DataFrame({"match_opponent": order}).merge(clean, on="match_opponent", how="left")

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=clean, x="match_opponent", y="clean_hold", order=order,
            hue="match_opponent", palette=result_palette, legend=False, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="clean hold rate",
       title="Clean holds - points scored without a turnover")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    v = clean.loc[clean["match_opponent"] == opp, "clean_hold"].iloc[0]
    ax.text(i, v + 0.01, f"{v:.0%}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Recovery rate

Percentage of points where there was at least one turnover and we scored (how much did we take the disc back).

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
to_pts = points[points["had_turnover"]].copy()
recovery = to_pts.groupby("match_opponent", as_index=False).agg(
    pts_with_to=("scored", "size"),
    recovered=("scored", "sum"),
)
recovery["recovery_rate"] = recovery["recovered"] / recovery["pts_with_to"]
recovery = pd.DataFrame({"match_opponent": order}).merge(recovery, on="match_opponent", how="left")
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=recovery, x="match_opponent", y="recovery_rate", order=order,
            hue="match_opponent", palette=result_palette, legend=False, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="recovery rate",
       title="Score rate on points that contained a turnover")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    v = recovery.loc[recovery["match_opponent"] == opp, "recovery_rate"].iloc[0]
    if pd.notna(v):
        ax.text(i, v + 0.01, f"{v:.0%}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

# Pass analysis

Passes below are flipped to be in a single offensive direction (left to right). A *huck* is a pass that is at least as long as 50% of the in-field (the field minus the two endzones).

## Red-zone turnover rate 

Completion rate of attacking passes into the endzone.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3
attempts = df[df["x2"] > FIELD_W - EZ_DEPTH].copy()
attempt_stats = attempts.groupby("match_opponent", as_index=False).agg(
    attempts=("pass_id", "count"),
    failed=("is_turnover", "sum"),
)
attempt_stats["fail_rate"] = attempt_stats["failed"] / attempt_stats["attempts"]
attempt_stats = pd.DataFrame({"match_opponent": order}).merge(attempt_stats, on="match_opponent", how="left").fillna(0)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
sns.barplot(data=attempt_stats, x="match_opponent", y="fail_rate", order=order, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="turnover rate",
       title="Turnover rate of passes into the endzone")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    row = attempt_stats[attempt_stats["match_opponent"] == opp].iloc[0]
    if row["attempts"] > 0:
        ax.text(i, row["fail_rate"] + 0.01,
                f"{row['fail_rate']:.0%} ({int(row['failed'])}/{int(row['attempts'])})",
                ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Hucks per game and their turnover rate
Same-third hucks are hucks that originate and end in the same third of the field, only the two border thirds are considered. 

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 3.6
_h = df[df["is_huck"]].copy()
_completed = int((_h["is_turnover"] == 0).sum())
_turned = int((_h["is_turnover"] == 1).sum())

# Same-third hucks: y1 and y2 fall in the same horizontal third of the field.
# Only the two sideline thirds count — the middle third is excluded.
third_edges = np.linspace(0, FIELD_H, 4)
_h["band1"] = np.digitize(_h["y1"], third_edges[1:-1])
_h["band2"] = np.digitize(_h["y2"], third_edges[1:-1])
_same = _h[(_h["band1"] == _h["band2"]) & (_h["band1"] != 1)]
_s_completed = int((_same["is_turnover"] == 0).sum())
_s_turned = int((_same["is_turnover"] == 1).sum())

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(7.2, 3.6))
for ax, vals, title in [
    (axes[0], (_completed, _turned), "Overall huck completion"),
    (axes[1], (_s_completed, _s_turned),
     f"Same-third hucks ({_s_completed + _s_turned}/{_completed + _turned})"),
]:
    c, t = vals
    ax.pie(
        [c, t],
        labels=[f"completed\n{c}", f"turnover\n{t}"],
        colors=["#2e7d32", "#c62828"],
        autopct="%1.0f%%",
        startangle=90,
        wedgeprops=dict(edgecolor="white", linewidth=1.5),
        textprops=dict(fontsize=10),
    )
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=11, loc="left")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.4
hucks = df[df["is_huck"]]
huck_stats = hucks.groupby("match_opponent", as_index=False).agg(
    hucks=("pass_id", "count"),
    huck_turnovers=("is_turnover", "sum"),
)
huck_stats["huck_to_rate"] = huck_stats["huck_turnovers"] / huck_stats["hucks"]
huck_stats = pd.DataFrame({"match_opponent": order}).merge(huck_stats, on="match_opponent", how="left").fillna(0)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(8, 3.4))
sns.barplot(data=huck_stats, x="match_opponent", y="hucks", order=order, ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set(xlabel="", ylabel="hucks", title="Hucks per match")

sns.barplot(data=huck_stats, x="match_opponent", y="huck_to_rate", order=order, ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set(xlabel="", ylabel="turnover rate", title="Huck turnover rate")
axes[1].yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
for i, opp in enumerate(order):
    v = huck_stats.loc[huck_stats["match_opponent"] == opp, "huck_to_rate"].iloc[0]
    axes[1].text(i, v + 0.01, f"{v:.0%}", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Huck map - completed vs. turnover

Again, passes are normalized so offense always attacks left to right. Completed vs turnover hucks.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 8.4
hucks_norm = df[df["is_huck"]].copy()
completed_hucks = hucks_norm[hucks_norm["is_turnover"] == 0]
turn_hucks = hucks_norm[hucks_norm["is_turnover"] == 1]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(8, 8.4))

for ax, sub, color, title in [
    (axes[0], completed_hucks, "#2e7d32", "completed hucks"),
    (axes[1], turn_hucks, "#c62828", "turnover hucks"),
]:
    draw_field(ax)
    for _, row in sub.iterrows():
        ax.annotate(
            "",
            xy=(row["x2"], row["y2"]), xytext=(row["x1"], row["y1"]),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=color, alpha=0.75, lw=1.3),
        )
    ax.scatter(sub["x1"], sub["y1"], s=18, color=color, marker="x", linewidths=1.2)
    ax.set_title(f"{title} (n={len(sub)})", fontsize=11, loc="left")

plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Huck completion rate by origin

Field divided into a grid, cells show how often a huck thrown from that area was completed. Cells with no hucks are gray. Labels hold the `completed / total` ratio.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.2
nx, ny = 5, 3
x_edges = np.linspace(0, FIELD_W, nx + 1)
y_edges = np.linspace(0, FIELD_H, ny + 1)

h_all, _, _ = np.histogram2d(hucks_norm["x1"], hucks_norm["y1"],
                             bins=[x_edges, y_edges])
completed_mask = hucks_norm["is_turnover"] == 0
h_done, _, _ = np.histogram2d(hucks_norm.loc[completed_mask, "x1"],
                              hucks_norm.loc[completed_mask, "y1"],
                              bins=[x_edges, y_edges])
with np.errstate(divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
    rate = np.where(h_all > 0, h_done / h_all, np.nan)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.2))
draw_field(ax)
cmap = plt.matplotlib.colormaps["RdYlGn"].copy()
cmap.set_bad("#eeeeee")
im = ax.imshow(rate.T, origin="upper", extent=[0, FIELD_W, FIELD_H, 0],
               vmin=0, vmax=1, cmap=cmap, alpha=0.65, aspect="equal", zorder=0)
for i in range(nx):
    for j in range(ny):
        n = int(h_all[i, j])
        if n == 0:
            continue
        cx = (x_edges[i] + x_edges[i + 1]) / 2
        cy = (y_edges[j] + y_edges[j + 1]) / 2
        ax.text(cx, cy, f"{int(h_done[i, j])}/{n}",
                ha="center", va="center", fontsize=10, color="#1a1a1a")
cbar = fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax, fraction=0.04, pad=0.02)
cbar.set_label("completion rate")
cbar.ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Turnover rate by pass direction (forward vs backward)

```{python}
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 3
df["direction_label"] = df["is_forward"].map({True: "forward", False: "backward"})
dir_stats = df.groupby("direction_label", as_index=False).agg(
    passes=("pass_id", "count"),
    turnovers=("is_turnover", "sum"),
)
dir_stats["to_rate"] = dir_stats["turnovers"] / dir_stats["passes"]
dir_stats = dir_stats.set_index("direction_label").reindex(["forward", "backward"]).reset_index()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 3))
sns.barplot(data=dir_stats, x="direction_label", y="to_rate", ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="turnover rate",
       title="Turnover rate - forward vs. backward")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
for i, row in dir_stats.iterrows():
    ax.text(i, row["to_rate"] + 0.003,
            f"{row['to_rate']:.0%} ({int(row['turnovers'])}/{int(row['passes'])})",
            ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Turnover breakdown - mechanical, huck, other

We noted when a turnover was mechanical (= a poorly executed throw, "osobní chyba, zához"), not a decision-making or system error or a good defensive effort.   

```{python}
#| fig-width: 5.5
#| fig-height: 3
turns = df[df["is_turnover"].astype(bool)].copy()
def classify(r):
    if r["is_mechanical"]:
        return "mechanical"
    if r["is_huck"]:
        return "huck"
    return "other"
turns["kind"] = turns.apply(classify, axis=1)
order_kind = ["mechanical", "huck", "other"]
breakdown = turns["kind"].value_counts().reindex(order_kind, fill_value=0)
share = breakdown / breakdown.sum() if breakdown.sum() else breakdown

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5.5, 3))
sns.barplot(x=share.index.tolist(), y=share.values, ax=ax)
ax.set(xlabel="", ylabel="share of turnovers",
       title="Breakdown of turnover causes")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.matplotlib.ticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
for i, (k, v) in enumerate(share.items()):
    ax.text(i, v + 0.01, f"{v:.0%} ({breakdown[k]})", ha="center", fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

# Turnover heat-maps

O is left to right again. Darker = more likely a turnover happens in that region. 

```{python}
#| label: heatmap-helpers
def draw_field(ax):
    ax.set_xlim(0, FIELD_W)
    ax.set_ylim(FIELD_H, 0)
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    ax.set_facecolor("white")
    ax.add_patch(plt.Rectangle((0, 0), FIELD_W, FIELD_H, fill=False, edgecolor="#1a1a1a", linewidth=1.2))
    ax.axvline(EZ_DEPTH, color="#1a1a1a", linewidth=0.8)
    ax.axvline(FIELD_W - EZ_DEPTH, color="#1a1a1a", linewidth=0.8)
    ax.annotate("", xy=(FIELD_W/2 + 35, -12), xytext=(FIELD_W/2 - 35, -12),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color="#888"),
                annotation_clip=False)
    ax.text(FIELD_W/2, -28, "offense", ha="center", fontsize=9, color="#888")
    ax.grid(False)
    ax.set_xticks([]); ax.set_yticks([])
    for s in ax.spines.values(): s.set_visible(False)

turns_df = df[df["is_turnover"].astype(bool)].copy()
```

## Origin - where turnovers are *thrown from*

```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.2
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.2))
draw_field(ax)
if len(turns_df) >= 3:
    sns.kdeplot(
        data=turns_df, x="x1", y="y1",
        fill=True, cmap="rocket_r", alpha=0.75, levels=12, thresh=0.05, ax=ax,
        bw_adjust=0.8, clip=((0, FIELD_W), (0, FIELD_H)),
    )
ax.scatter(turns_df["x1"], turns_df["y1"], s=18, color="#1a1a1a", marker="x", linewidths=1.2)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Destination - where passes that are turnovers *are thrown to*

The intended (or actual) target location of every turnover pass.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.2
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.2))
draw_field(ax)
if len(turns_df) >= 3:
    sns.kdeplot(
        data=turns_df, x="x2", y="y2",
        fill=True, cmap="rocket_r", alpha=0.75, levels=12, thresh=0.05, ax=ax,
        bw_adjust=0.8, clip=((0, FIELD_W), (0, FIELD_H)),
    )
ax.scatter(turns_df["x2"], turns_df["y2"], s=22, color="#1a1a1a", marker="o",
           facecolors="none", linewidths=1.2)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Arrows - every turnover 

Each turnover pass drawn as a red arrow from origin to destination, with the attacking direction unified to the right.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.2
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.2))
draw_field(ax)
for _, row in turns_df.iterrows():
    ax.annotate(
        "",
        xy=(row["x2"], row["y2"]), xytext=(row["x1"], row["y1"]),
        arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color="#c62828", alpha=0.75, lw=1.3),
    )
ax.scatter(turns_df["x1"], turns_df["y1"], s=16, color="#c62828",
           marker="x", linewidths=1.2)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Turnover origin by field quarter

Field split into four horizontal quarters; each shaded by the share of turnovers whose origin are inside it.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.2
quarter_edges = np.linspace(0, FIELD_H, 5)
counts, _ = np.histogram(turns_df["y1"], bins=quarter_edges)
total = counts.sum()
ratios = counts / total if total else counts.astype(float)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.2))
draw_field(ax)
cmap = plt.matplotlib.colormaps["Reds"]
vmin, vmax = ratios.min(), ratios.max()
span = vmax - vmin
for i in range(4):
    y0, y1 = quarter_edges[i], quarter_edges[i + 1]
    # Stretch [vmin, vmax] across [0.12, 0.95] for max contrast between strips.
    t = (ratios[i] - vmin) / span if span else 0.5
    color = cmap(0.12 + 0.83 * t)
    ax.add_patch(plt.Rectangle((0, y0), FIELD_W, y1 - y0,
                               color=color, alpha=0.85, zorder=0))
    ax.text(FIELD_W / 2, (y0 + y1) / 2,
            f"{ratios[i]:.0%}  ({counts[i]}/{total})",
            ha="center", va="center", fontsize=11, color="#1a1a1a")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

## Pass completion rate by field quarter

Field split into four horizontal quarters; each shaded by the completion rate of passes whose origin are inside it.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4.2
quarter_edges = np.linspace(0, FIELD_H, 5)
all_counts, _ = np.histogram(df["y1"], bins=quarter_edges)
to_counts, _ = np.histogram(df.loc[df["is_turnover"] == 1, "y1"], bins=quarter_edges)
completed = all_counts - to_counts
with np.errstate(divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
    comp_rate = np.where(all_counts > 0, completed / all_counts, np.nan)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.2))
draw_field(ax)
cmap = plt.matplotlib.colormaps["Blues"]
valid = comp_rate[~np.isnan(comp_rate)]
vmin, vmax = (valid.min(), valid.max()) if len(valid) else (0, 1)
span = vmax - vmin
for i in range(4):
    y0, y1 = quarter_edges[i], quarter_edges[i + 1]
    if np.isnan(comp_rate[i]):
        color = (0.93, 0.93, 0.93, 1.0)
        label = "no data"
    else:
        t = (comp_rate[i] - vmin) / span if span else 0.5
        color = cmap(0.12 + 0.83 * t)
        label = f"{comp_rate[i]:.0%}  ({int(completed[i])}/{int(all_counts[i])})"
    ax.add_patch(plt.Rectangle((0, y0), FIELD_W, y1 - y0,
                               color=color, alpha=0.85, zorder=0))
    ax.text(FIELD_W / 2, (y0 + y1) / 2, label,
            ha="center", va="center", fontsize=11, color="#1a1a1a")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```


# Pass-distance distribution

Distribution of pass horizontal span - completed vs. turnovers. Dashed line marks the huck threshold.

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.2
df["abs_dx"] = df["dx"].abs()
xmax = df["abs_dx"].max() * 1.05

palette = sns.color_palette("deep")
completed_color = palette[0]
turnover_color = palette[3]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(7, 5.2), sharex=True)

# Completed
sns.histplot(
    data=df[df["is_turnover"] == 0], x="abs_dx",
    stat="count", kde=True, bins=40, alpha=0.5,
    color=completed_color, ax=axes[0],
)
axes[0].set(xlabel="horizontal span (px) - pass length", ylabel="count", title="completed passes")
axes[0].yaxis.set_major_locator(plt.matplotlib.ticker.MaxNLocator(integer=True))
axes[0].axvline(HUCK_THRESHOLD, linestyle="--", color="#888", linewidth=0.8)
axes[0].text(HUCK_THRESHOLD + 5, axes[0].get_ylim()[1] * 0.85, "huck", color="#888", fontsize=9)

# Turnovers
sns.histplot(
    data=df[df["is_turnover"] == 1], x="abs_dx",
    stat="count", kde=True, bins=40, alpha=0.5,
    color=turnover_color, ax=axes[1],
)
axes[1].set(xlabel="horizontal span (px) - pass length", ylabel="count", title="turnovers")
axes[1].yaxis.set_major_locator(plt.matplotlib.ticker.MaxNLocator(integer=True))
axes[1].axvline(HUCK_THRESHOLD, linestyle="--", color="#888", linewidth=0.8)
axes[1].text(HUCK_THRESHOLD + 5, axes[1].get_ylim()[1] * 0.85, "huck", color="#888", fontsize=9)

for ax in axes:
    ax.set_xlim(0, xmax)

plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```
